what_it_does: Defines placeholder metadata for unbuilt design-system canvases and renders the selected canvas by id with a header, descriptive stub copy, feature list, and two action buttons, then exposes StubCanvas on window.
observations: STUB_DEFS contains definitions for type, logos, imagery, components, templates, and voice canvases.
Each definition includes title, sub, desc, and a features array.
StubCanvas looks up a definition by id and renders a plain unknown-canvas fallback when no definition exists.
The rendered stub uses CanvasHeader, ViShape, dsa-* class names, and a feature list marked with diamond symbols.
The file assigns StubCanvas to window instead of exporting it as a module.
standouts: Data-driven placeholder rendering keeps multiple canvas stubs in one component.
The stub copy frames future canvases as Vi-assisted generation and asset management surfaces.
conventions: Uses a PascalCase React component function.
Uses an object keyed by canvas id to drive UI content.
Uses a fragment wrapper around the rendered canvas content.
Relies on globally available CanvasHeader, ViShape, and window assignment rather than local imports or exports.
concerns: CanvasHeader and ViShape are referenced without imports or local definitions.
The component is attached to window, which couples usage to a global runtime.
There is inline style on the button container.
Feature list keys use array indexes.
notes: This file is a data-driven React stub renderer for several unbuilt ConceptV design-system canvases.
questions: Where are CanvasHeader and ViShape provided from in the runtime?
Is window.StubCanvas the intended registration mechanism for this app?
Should the action buttons have handlers when these canvases become active?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated placeholder-canvas purpose matches the implementation: it scaffolds not-yet-built surfaces with descriptive metadata and non-functional UI affordances.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make unfinished design-system areas navigable and understandable while indicating the planned workflows for each future canvas.
novelty: The placeholder content is unusually specific, describing future Vi-assisted workflows for each canvas rather than using generic coming-soon text.
contribution: Contributes a compact ledger of planned design-system surfaces, their user-facing descriptions, and expected feature directions that a fused system could preserve as placeholder navigation and intent metadata.
summary_for_embedding: A React stub canvas file defining metadata for six ConceptV design-system areas and rendering the selected placeholder with header, copy, feature rows, and action buttons.
intention_for_embedding: This was for exposing unfinished design-system canvases in the UI while documenting the intended role and future capabilities of each surface.

symbols.does: Renders a placeholder canvas for a given id using STUB_DEFS metadata.
symbols.description: StubCanvas accepts an id, retrieves the matching definition from STUB_DEFS, and displays its title, subtitle, description, feature list, and two stub action buttons. If the id is not defined, it returns a simple unknown-canvas message. The component depends on CanvasHeader and ViShape being available in the surrounding runtime.
